chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Has Been Narrated Regarding the Disliked Nature of Traveling in the Month of Ramadan

text: 1968 - رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلْخُرُوجِ إِذَا دَخَلَ شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ فَقَالَ «لاَ إِلاَّ فِيمَا أُخْبِرُكَ بِهِ خُرُوجٌ إِلَى مَكَّةَ أَوْ غَزْوٌ فِي سَبِيلِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَوْ مَالٌ تَخَافُ هَلاَكَهُ أَوْ أَخٌ تَخَافُ هَلاَكَهُ وَ أَنَّهُ لَيْسَ بِأَخٍ مِنَ اَلْأَبِ وَ اَلْأُمِّ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1968 - Ali ibn Abi Hamzah narrated from Abu Basir, who said:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about traveling once the Month of Ramadan has begun.
Imam (as) said: 'Do not [travel], except in cases I will inform you of: traveling to Mecca, waging battle in the way of Allah (swt) Almighty, preserving wealth you fear will perish, or saving a brother you fear will face destruction—and he is not a brother from the same father and mother.'"
